docs: https://docs.breezometer.com/api-documentation/ (live docs.breezometer.com now 301-redirects to Google Maps Platform's Environment product marketing page since BreezoMeter's core Air Quality/Pollen data model was absorbed into Google's Air Quality/Pollen APIs; api.breezometer.com itself remains a live, separately-branded endpoint — verified via Wayback Machine snapshots of the original per-product doc pages: air-quality-api/v2 20250305180416, weather-api/v1 20230128173056, pollen-api/v2 20251105075108, cleanest-route-api/v1 20251025033220, environmental-alerts-api/v1 20251101153919, wildfire-tracker-api/v1 20251102165056)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: cross-checked every api.breezometer.com product's own documentation page (Air Quality v2, Pollen v2, Weather v1, Wildfire Tracker+ v1, Cleanest Route v1, Environmental Alerts v1, Heatmap Tile Overlay) rather than assuming the wave2 5-stream set was already complete. Air Quality v2's real documented surface is EXACTLY 3 endpoints (Current Conditions/Hourly Forecast/Hourly History) — the wave2-era `air-quality/v2/historical/daily` and `air-quality/v2/road-segments` placeholder entries do not correspond to any real, currently-documented BreezoMeter endpoint and are corrected below rather than carried forward as generic 'Pass B' placeholders. Pollen v2's real surface is EXACTLY 1 endpoint (Daily Forecast), already covered. Weather v1 DOES have a real, previously-missed 3rd endpoint (`forecast/daily`), now added as the `weather_daily_forecast` stream. Wildfire Tracker+'s Precision Tracking sub-product (2 self-serve GET endpoints, `lat`/`lon`/`radius`-scoped like every other stream in this bundle) is added as 2 new streams; its Area Monitoring sub-product requires a contract-level, account-manager-configured area-of-interest tied to the API key itself (not a runtime parameter this connector's config surface can express) and is excluded. Cleanest Route (a stateless POST scoring computation over caller-supplied route polylines, not a persistent-object mutation) is added as a write action. Environmental Alerts (an out-of-band webhook-registration platform, not a synchronous request/response data operation) and Heatmap Tile Overlay (returns raw PNG tile images, not JSON) are excluded.

excluded.reason: Wildfire Tracker+ Area Monitoring's areas of interest are predefined at the CONTRACT level (the API key itself is scoped to a fixed set of areas configured by a BreezoMeter account manager) — there is no lat/lon/radius runtime parameter this connector's config surface can express to select a different area; Precision Tracking's locate-and-track endpoint (self-serve, lat/lon/radius-scoped) covers the equivalent 'find active fires near a point' capability instead

same contract-level predefined-area-of-interest gating as /fires/v1/area-monitoring/active (Braze's own docs note this endpoint was not yet generally available at time of documentation); Precision Tracking's burnt-area endpoint (self-serve, lat/lon/radius-scoped) covers the equivalent capability instead

the Environmental Alerts Platform is an out-of-band webhook-registration and asynchronous-delivery system (requires a pre-configured webhook token and alert-sensitivity settings via a separate setup flow) — its own docs describe a fundamentally different request/response contract than every other endpoint in this bundle, not a synchronous data read or a plain-body create/update mutation this dialect's write model fits
returns a raw 256x256 PNG heatmap tile image, not a JSON record; also served from a distinct tiles.breezometer.com host rather than api.breezometer.com
